empty_state.welcome_description: Select a section from the left menu to start working with agents and flows

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.hero_subtitle: Platform for creating smart AI agents and automating business processes
welcome.intro_text: We are glad to welcome you to the Humanitec platform! Here you can create intelligent bots, automate routine tasks and connect them to your favorite communication channels - all without a single line of code.

create_bots.title: Create bots easily
create_bots.description: Visual editor and ready-made templates will allow you to launch your first bot in a matter of minutes

builder.title: Builder for complex scenarios
builder.description: Create multi-agent systems using a visual constructor and customize the logic of operation

channels.title: Multiple channels
channels.description: Connect Telegram, WhatsApp, Web Chat, API and AmoCRM - all in one place

rag.title: Knowledge Base (RAG)
rag.description: Upload documents and your bot will answer based on your data

tools.title: Rich set of tools
tools.description: Integrations with external services, file operations, API and much more

analytics.title: Analytics and monitoring
analytics.description: Track bot effectiveness, view conversation history and optimize performance

cta.title: Get started right now!
cta.text: Create your first bot or choose a ready-made solution from the store

1.title: Go to the "Bots" section
1.description: Create your first bot or choose a template from the store

2.title: Configure prompt and capabilities
2.description: Describe what your bot should do and add the necessary tools

3.title: Connect channels
3.description: Add Telegram, WhatsApp or other communication channels

4.title: Test and launch
4.description: Test the bot in the built-in chat and launch in production

tracing_page.unavailable: PostgreSQL tracing is not available in this environment
tracing_page.facet_hint: Type at least 2 characters for suggestions; picking a row sets an exact match for search

platform_billing_page.title: Billing & usage (admin)
platform_billing_page.subtitle: Global catalog and company prices in one section; settlement rules are per company; usage report.
platform_billing_page.hint_billing_page_about: Global catalog and company prices in one section; settlement rules are per company; usage report. The company selected at the top drives settlement rules, per-company prices (inside «Price catalog»), and the usage report filter.
platform_billing_page.billing_scope_none_hint: No company selected: the per-company price block inside «Price catalog» is disabled; settlement rules and the usage report below also need a company.
platform_billing_page.hint_billing_company_scope: The same company selection drives: settlement rules (this company only), per-company prices (inside «Price catalog»), and the usage report filter. Global override and effective catalog are not tied to a company.
platform_billing_page.hint_billing_company_pick: Company list and search by name, slug, and id. If missing from the list, type slug or id in the search field and press Enter. Enter in the search field resolves by slug or company_id if the company is not in the list.
platform_billing_page.billing_company_list_empty: No matches — type slug or id and press Enter
platform_billing_page.billing_company_enter_resolve: Enter in the search field resolves by slug or company_id if the company is not in the list.
platform_billing_page.billing_company_required: Select a company and load settings (button above) first.
platform_billing_page.hint_prices_global_scope: Global catalog override: applies to every company unless the company has its own price override.
platform_billing_page.hint_price_catalog_table: Full merged prices (config + override) are read-only; edit the global override in the block above.
platform_billing_page.hint_prices_override_only: Only the rows below are persisted. An empty list means no global override; prices come from config/defaults. Each row overrides one catalog entry for all companies without their own price set.
platform_billing_page.hint_prices_override_rows: Each row overrides one catalog entry for all companies without their own price set.
platform_billing_page.hint_settlement_company_scope: After you pick a company, its rules are loaded from storage here.
platform_billing_page.hint_settlement_per_company_only: Rules are not platform-wide: each company has its own document in storage (billing:company:{company_id}:settlement_rules_json). You only see and edit rules for the selected company.
platform_billing_page.hint_rules_why_few: A short list means that is what is stored for this company (migration from legacy global key or a manual save). For the full code template: use «Insert platform template», then «Save rules».
platform_billing_page.hint_company_prices_scope: Price override only for the company selected above.
platform_billing_page.hint_company_prices_in_catalog: Overrides global catalog and global storage override for the company chosen in «Company» at the top.
platform_billing_page.forbidden: This section is only available when the active company is system
platform_billing_page.rules_count_hint: This company’s document in storage currently has {{count}} rules.
platform_billing_page.confirm_replace_rules_default: Replace the current rules in the form with the full platform default? Unsaved edits will be lost until you save.
platform_billing_page.rules_template_loaded: Template inserted into the form. Click Save rules to persist for the company.
platform_billing_page.hint_prices_section: Global catalog override applies to every company unless the company has its own price override. The full merged catalog (config + override) in «Effective catalog» is read-only; edit the global override using the rows below. Base prices for billing resources. Category groups resources (e.g. llm, embedding, livekit). Resource is a specific item within the category. Use * as a wildcard to set the default price for a category. Prices are used when the settlement job runs. Flows tool invocations are not priced here.
platform_billing_page.hint_price_category: Resource group: llm (language models), embedding (vector embeddings), livekit (video/audio calls).
platform_billing_page.hint_price_resource: Specific resource within the category. Use * for the default price when no exact match is found.
platform_billing_page.hint_price_price: Cost per unit (e.g. tokens for LLM). Applied when the settlement job converts spans to usage records.
platform_billing_page.hint_settlement_section: The settlement job runs periodically, finds traced spans marked for billing and creates usage records. These rules define how to match spans and what to charge. Rules are not platform-wide: each company has its own document in storage (key billing:company:{company_id}:settlement_rules_json); you only see and edit rules for the selected company. Rules apply to spans whose company_id matches when the settlement job runs. The count next to the header is how many rules are in that document. If the document is missing or has no rules, the platform code default is written on load (frontend startup or API fetch). Manually: «Insert platform template», then «Save rules».
platform_billing_page.hint_rule_id: Unique identifier for this rule. Used for tracking which rule generated a usage record.
platform_billing_page.hint_resource_name: Billing resource in format category:name. Determines the price lookup. E.g. llm:gpt-4, livekit:room_create.
platform_billing_page.hint_usage_type: Type of usage record created: llm_request, tool_call (e.g. livekit), embedding_request, etc.
platform_billing_page.hint_priority: Lower number = higher priority. When multiple rules match the same span, priority determines which fires first.
platform_billing_page.hint_exclusive_group: Rules in the same group are mutually exclusive. Only the highest-priority match within a group fires. Leave empty if the rule is independent.
platform_billing_page.hint_quantity_from: Where to get the quantity value. const:N for a fixed number, attr:KEY to read from a span attribute (e.g. attr:platform.llm.total_tokens for token count).
platform_billing_page.hint_application_mode: all_matching: every matching rule creates a usage record. first_win: only the first matching rule (by priority) fires per span.
platform_billing_page.hint_match_op_prefix: Match spans whose operation name starts with this prefix. E.g. flows.llm matches all LLM operations.
platform_billing_page.hint_match_op_equals: Match spans whose operation name is exactly this value. E.g. flows.mcp.call_tool.
platform_billing_page.hint_match_service: Match spans from a specific service: flows, sync, rag, crm, frontend, scheduler.
platform_billing_page.hint_match_event_type: Match spans with a specific event type. E.g. llm.call, external_api.call, channel.action.
platform_billing_page.hint_match_attrs: Additional span attribute filters. Key must match exactly. E.g. platform.channel.type = telegram.
platform_billing_page.hint_company_prices: Override prices only for the company selected in «Company» at the top. Takes precedence over the global catalog and global storage override for that company.
platform_billing_page.hint_usage_section: Search usage records created by the settlement job. Filter by company, type, resource or date range. In filter fields: type at least 2 characters for suggestions; pick a row to fill the exact value (same as platform tracing).
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_company: Filter by company ID. Leave empty to see all companies.
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_usage_type: Filter by usage type: llm_request, tool_call, embedding_request, etc.
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_resource: Filter by resource name, e.g. llm:gpt-4 or livekit:room_create.
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_from: Show records created after this date/time.
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_to: Show records created before this date/time.
platform_billing_page.hint_filter_limit: Maximum number of records to return per page (1–5000).
platform_billing_page.usage_facet_hint: Type at least 2 characters for suggestions; pick a row to fill the exact value (same as platform tracing).

api_key_modal.warning_line1: Store this key in a safe place.
api_key_modal.warning_line2: It will not be shown again.
